skill.name: parallel-task-spark
skill.description: Orchestrates parallel AI subagents to execute development tasks from a plan file. Manages task dependencies, launches tasks in waves, validates completion, and logs progress. Use when you need to coordinate multiple AI agents working on different parts of a project simultaneously.
skill.summary: Orchestrate parallel AI subagents to execute development tasks with dependency management
skill.author: am-will

security_audit.summary: This is a legitimate agent orchestration skill. Static findings for external_commands, weak cryptographic algorithms, and system reconnaissance are false positives. The skill contains markdown instructions for coordinating subagents, not executable code. No network requests, no shell execution, no sensitive data access.

high_findings.title: External Commands Pattern (False Positive)
high_findings.description: Static analyzer detected 'backtick execution' pattern in markdown. This is a false positive - the skill contains markdown instructions with task IDs (e.g., T1, T2) and dependency references that the analyzer misinterpreted as shell commands. No actual command execution occurs.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill is pure markdown with orchestration instructions. Task identifiers like 'T1' and 'depends_on' are configuration data, not executable commands.

high_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm (False Positive)
high_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm'. This is a false positive - there is no cryptographic code in this skill. The analyzer likely misidentified other patterns.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: No encryption, hashing, or cryptographic functions exist in this skill. This is a simple orchestration skill.

high_findings.title: System Reconnaissance (False Positive)
high_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'system reconnaissance' at line 40. This is a false positive - the line contains instructions to launch subagents with agent_type: sparky, which is legitimate orchestration, not reconnaissance.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reference to 'agent_type: sparky' is configuration for launching subagents, not system scanning or reconnaissance.

content.value_statement: Managing multiple AI subagents working on a project is challenging when tracking dependencies and ensuring proper execution order. This skill orchestrates parallel task execution with dependency management, validation, and progress logging.
actual_capabilities: Parse development plan files to extract task definitions with dependencies
Launch subagents in parallel waves based on task dependency readiness
Validate task completion and ensure proper logging in plan documents
Manage task retry logic for failed or incomplete tasks
Coordinate multiple AI agents working simultaneously on different project parts
limitations: Requires a properly formatted plan file with task definitions
Cannot execute tasks that require interactive user input during execution
Relies on subagents following the sparky role specification

use_cases.title: Multi-file Refactoring
use_cases.description: Execute a large refactoring plan that involves changing multiple files with interdependencies. Launch refactoring tasks in parallel where safe, then sequential where dependencies exist.
use_cases.target_user: Developers working on large codebase refactoring

use_cases.title: Feature Implementation Coordination
use_cases.description: Implement a complex feature that spans multiple modules by breaking it into dependent tasks and coordinating parallel implementation by multiple subagents.

use_cases.title: Parallel Testing and Validation
use_cases.description: Run multiple test suites or validation checks in parallel, then aggregate results and retry failed tests automatically.
use_cases.target_user: QA engineers running comprehensive test campaigns

prompt_templates.title: Basic Plan Execution
prompt_templates.prompt: Execute the development plan in plan.md using the parallel-task-spark skill.
prompt_templates.scenario: Run all tasks from a plan file

prompt_templates.title: Specific Task Subset
prompt_templates.prompt: Using /parallel-task-spark, run tasks T1, T2, and T4 from auth-plan.md
prompt_templates.scenario: Run only specific tasks from a plan

prompt_templates.title: Filtered Task Execution
prompt_templates.prompt: Execute only tasks T3 and T7 from user-profile-plan.md using parallel-task-spark
prompt_templates.scenario: Run a subset of tasks with their dependencies

prompt_templates.title: Full Project Coordination
prompt_templates.prompt: Coordinate the full implementation of our microservices migration plan. Parse the plan, launch all unblocked tasks in parallel, validate completion, and ensure each wave is committed before proceeding.

output_examples.output:
Launching 3 parallel subagents for unblocked tasks T1, T2, T3...

T1 completed: Created user model
T2 completed: Added authentication controller
T3 completed: Implemented login endpoint

Wave 1 committed. Checking for unblocked tasks...

Filtering to task subset: T1, T5
Dependencies: T1 has no deps, T5 depends on T3
Including T3 as required dependency

Launching wave 1: T1, T3
Launching wave 2: T5

best_practices: Ensure plan files have clear task dependencies defined using depends_on fields
Keep tasks atomic and focused for better parallelization opportunities
Review plan logs after each wave to track progress and identify blockers
anti_patterns: Creating tasks with circular dependencies that prevent execution
Setting overly broad task scopes that require too much context
Skipping validation steps and marking tasks complete without verification

faq.question: What format should my plan file use?
faq.answer: Plans should use markdown with task subsections marked by ### Task ID. Each task needs a depends_on list, description, location, acceptance criteria, and validation steps.

faq.question: Can I run only specific tasks from a plan?
faq.answer: Yes, provide task IDs after the plan file path. The skill will run only those tasks plus any dependencies required for them.

faq.question: How does the skill handle task failures?
faq.answer: The orchestrator checks task completion after each wave. Failed tasks are identified and can be retried or escalated.

faq.question: What is the sparky agent role?
faq.answer: Sparky is a specific subagent role defined by this skill. All tasks must use this role for proper orchestration.

faq.question: Does the skill commit changes automatically?
faq.answer: Subagents commit their work after each task but do not push. The orchestrator ensures each wave is committed before proceeding.

faq.question: Can I use this with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill supports claude, codex, and claude-code tools as specified in the skill metadata.
